lessons.title: Playwright request Fixture from beforeAll Cannot Be Reused in Test Body
lessons.context: Playwright's built-in request fixture is scoped to the test function. When you try to capture it in beforeAll and reuse it in test cases, Playwright throws an error because the fixture lifecycle doesn't allow cross-scope reuse. This is a common mistake when migrating from other frameworks.

problem.error: Fixture { request } from beforeAll cannot be reused in a test
problem.pattern: Fixture.*from beforeAll cannot be reused|cannot be reused in a test
problem.cause: Playwright's request fixture is test-scoped — it cannot be captured in beforeAll and passed to test bodies

antipattern.description: Capturing Playwright's built-in request fixture in beforeAll
antipattern.why_bad: The fixture is test-scoped — Playwright destroys it after each test. Reusing it across tests causes lifecycle errors.
code: // BAD — request fixture captured in beforeAll
req = request; // This will throw when used in test

solution.description: Create APIRequestContext manually using playwright.request.newContext() in beforeAll and dispose it in afterAll.

lessons.ai_instruction: When generating API test suites that need a shared request context, always use playwrightRequest.newContext() in beforeAll with a matching dispose() in afterAll. Never capture the built-in request fixture in beforeAll.
